Juncellus distachyos, also known as saltwort, is a halophytic succulent plant that thrives in coastal areas with high salt concentrations. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Juncellus distachyos include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin irritations.
